summary: READ-ONLY verification of production deployment + custom-domain (captureagent.us) failure evidence. Confirmed: (a) production deployment at https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host is LIVE and healthy, (b) frontend login page renders with all data-testids, (c) captureagent.us is NOT linked to Emergent — but the actual failure mode differs from the task description: TLS handshake SUCCEEDS (Cloudflare presents a valid Google Trust Services cert issued 2026-07-04), and Cloudflare returns HTTP/2 403 with body 'error code: 1034' indicating no origin route is configured on the Cloudflare side. Preview regression confirmed intact.

2_production_frontend_login_page.console_errors: One 401 from /api/auth/me (expected — unauthenticated visitor on login page)

dns_resolution.note: Both IPs are Cloudflare edge IPs — matches task expectation

tls_handshake.correction: TLS actually SUCCEEDS. Cloudflare has provisioned a valid cert (issued today, 2026-07-04). Task's stated failure mode is incorrect.

3_custom_domain_captureagent_us.conclusion: captureagent.us IS routed to Cloudflare and TLS works, but Cloudflare returns error 1034 ('Edge IP Restricted' / no valid origin route) — meaning the domain is registered on Cloudflare but the origin route to the Emergent deployment (govcon-workspace.emergent.host) is NOT configured. This still proves the domain is NOT linked to the Emergent deployment, but the fix path is different from a raw TLS failure.

raw_evidence.captureagent_us_curl_summary: curl -vI https://captureagent.us --> Connected to 172.66.2.113:443, TLSv1.3 handshake OK, server cert CN=captureagent.us issued by Google Trust Services (WE1), verified OK, HTTP/2 403, server: cloudflare, body: 'error code: 1034' (17 bytes), CF-RAY: a15f90722f1e1195-SIN
raw_evidence.captureagent_us_http_curl_summary: curl -vI http://captureagent.us --> 403 Forbidden from Server: cloudflare (same body)

action_items: IMPORTANT for user-facing report: The main agent's original diagnosis said 'SSL handshake fails' — this is INCORRECT. TLS handshake actually SUCCEEDS with a valid Google Trust Services cert (issued today, 2026-07-04). The actual failure is Cloudflare error 1034 (no origin route). Update user communication to reflect: 'captureagent.us is registered on Cloudflare and has a valid SSL certificate, but the origin route pointing to the Emergent deployment has not been fully configured/propagated. Complete the Link Domain → Entri flow in the Emergent UI (or verify the CNAME/route in Cloudflare points to govcon-workspace.emergent.host).'

The fact that the cert notBefore is 2026-07-04 (today) strongly suggests the user has already STARTED the Entri linking flow — the cert was just provisioned. The next step (origin route configuration) may still be propagating. Ask the user to wait 5–15 minutes and retry, or re-run the Link Domain flow in Emergent UI.
No code changes needed. This is a platform/DNS/Cloudflare configuration issue, not an app bug.
critical_code_review_comments: No code was modified or reviewed in this iteration (verification-only task). Backend and frontend from iteration_4 remain healthy in both preview and production.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (2/2 login pages render with all 3 testids visible — production + preview)
success_rate.custom_domain: 0% (captureagent.us returns Cloudflare 403 error 1034 — not linked to origin)

test_credentials: N/A — READ-ONLY verification, no login attempted (per instructions). /app/memory/test_credentials.md exists and is untouched.
seed_data_creation: None — no data created on production or preview (READ-ONLY compliance verified).
context_for_next_testing_agent: This was a platform-side custom-domain verification with no code changes. Deployment is healthy (backend + frontend). captureagent.us is on Cloudflare with a valid cert but returns error 1034 (no origin route). No re-test needed unless user re-attempts the Emergent Link Domain / Entri flow and reports a different failure mode.

rca_of_the_issue.user_reported: 'I can't access captureagent.us. Are you sure you deployed it?'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The Emergent deployment at https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host IS live and fully functional. The user's issue is that the custom domain captureagent.us is registered on Cloudflare (DNS + SSL cert provisioned today, 2026-07-04) but the origin route/CNAME pointing to the Emergent deployment has NOT been configured (Cloudflare error 1034 = no valid origin). This is a platform/DNS configuration gap, not an application bug.
reproduction_steps: 3. TLS cert for captureagent.us is valid (Google Trust Services, issued 2026-07-04) but Cloudflare has no origin to forward to
mitigation_steps: 1. In the Emergent UI: complete the 'Link Domain' / Entri flow for captureagent.us — this configures the Cloudflare origin route to point to govcon-workspace.emergent.host
2. If already initiated: wait 5–15 minutes for DNS/route propagation and retry
3. Verify in Cloudflare dashboard (if user has access) that the CNAME or origin route for captureagent.us points to govcon-workspace.emergent.host and 'Proxy status' is orange-cloud enabled
4. Once the origin route is live, https://captureagent.us should return the CaptureAgent login page (same as https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host)
